articles.title: The ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Movement Is Cooling on Trump and Republicans
articles.summary: Vaccine skeptics, ‘organic moms’ and anti-pesticide activists came together to elect President Trump. But some voters are disillusioned and might not turn out again.

articles.title: Microsoft Targets About 7% of Its U.S. Workers With Buyout Offer
articles.summary: The tech giant is offering long-serving employees early retirements as it continues to invest aggressively in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
articles.summary: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Meta will lay off 10% of its staff
articles.summary: The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
articles.summary: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished soon.

articles.title: OpenAI Unveils Its New, More Powerful GPT-5.5 Model
articles.summary: The maker of ChatGPT is taking a more open approach to cybersecurity than its chief rival, Anthropic.

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s Epic Fight Heads to Court
articles.summary: A jury trial that is set to start on Monday could shift the course of the A.I. race, with Mr. Musk seeking billions of dollars in damages from Mr. Altman’s OpenAI.

articles.title: Author details the spy network that took on America's post-WWII Nazi groups
articles.summary: In The Secret War Against Hate, Steven J. Ross details the racist, anti-Semitic groups that sprang up in the latter half of the 20th century — and the spy network that worked to bring them to justice.

articles.title: House Republicans Ask ActBlue C.E.O., Regina Wallace-Jones, to Testify
articles.summary: Hours after Republican lawmakers asked the Democratic fund-raising group’s chief executive to testify, ActBlue sent a sharp letter dismissing the inquiry as a “partisan attack.”

articles.title: Can a mentalist trick Trump? Oz Pearlman will try in a room full of journalists
articles.summary: The White House Correspondents' Dinner will be headlined by a mentalist instead of a comedian. Oz Pearlman tells NPR he hopes to unify, delight and puzzle the crowd — but can't reveal how.

articles.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
articles.summary: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

articles.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
articles.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketamine, Tylenol with codeine and anabolic steroids.

articles.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
articles.summary: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

articles.title: How People in Lebanon Are Reacting to Talks With Israel
articles.summary: The Lebanese are deeply divided over whether rare direct talks with Israel are a good thing.

articles.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
articles.summary: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

articles.title: Trump will welcome envoys of Lebanon and Israel at the White House for talks
articles.summary: Israel and Lebanon will hold talks at the White House as the countries' temporary ceasefire comes under strain from continued violence.

articles.title: Peace talks between U.S. and Iran at a standstill as Trump extends ceasefire
articles.summary: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.

articles.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
articles.summary: The executive director of World Press Photo said the image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separ

articles.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
articles.summary: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

articles.title: Candidates vying for U.N. secretary-general role face questions
articles.summary: Who will replace U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres when he steps down this year? Four candidates field questions from member states this week at U.N. headquarters.

articles.title: Putin's approval rating falls amid state-imposed internet restrictions
articles.summary: In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions — and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
